fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 63 is inverted. The clamping logic is incorrectly executed when `clamp` is `False` instead of when it is `True`.

spec.expr: (clamp and ((y__3 >= y0 and y__3 <= y1) or (y__3 >= y1 and y__3 <= y0))) or (not clamp and y__3 == y__1)

constraints.intent: The value of y after the conditional should be clamped if and only if clamping is enabled.
When the non-clamping branch is taken, it should only be because clamping is on and the value is already within the valid range.
When the clamping branch is (wrongly) taken, it implies no change should have been made to y because clamping was disabled.

spec.expr: not clamp or ((__cbfl_result >= y0 and __cbfl_result <= y1) or (__cbfl_result >= y1 and __cbfl_result <= y0))

constraints.intent: If clamping is enabled, the final result must be within the range defined by y0 and y1.
